name: DGTSClinicalSpecialtyGenomics
title: DGTS Clinical Specialty Genomics
description: CodeSystem used to identify the clinical specialty associated with a genomic test request, result, care pathway, or service within the Digital Genomic Test Service (DGTS).
purpose: Defines a standard set of clinical specialties relevant to genomic testing and interpretation within NHS genomic services.

concept.definition: Clinical specialty concerned with the diagnosis, treatment and management of cancer.
Clinical specialty concerned with disorders of the heart and cardiovascular system.
Clinical specialty concerned with hearing, balance and related disorders.
Clinical specialty concerned with hormone-producing glands and endocrine disorders.
Clinical specialty concerned with diseases and disorders of the eye and visual system.
Clinical specialty concerned with disorders of the gastrointestinal tract, liver and associated organs.
Clinical specialty concerned with blood disorders and diseases of the blood-forming organs.
Clinical specialty concerned with disorders of the immune system.
Clinical specialty focused on inherited cancer syndromes and familial cancer risk assessment.
Clinical specialty concerned with inherited and acquired metabolic disorders.
Clinical specialty concerned with disorders caused by mitochondrial dysfunction or mitochondrial genetic variants.
Clinical specialty concerned with disorders affecting bones, muscles, connective tissues and joints.
Clinical specialty concerned with disorders of the nervous system.
Clinical specialty concerned with kidney diseases and disorders of renal function.
Clinical specialty concerned with diseases of the lungs and respiratory system.
Clinical specialty concerned with disorders of the skin, hair and nails.
Clinical specialty concerned with fetal medicine, prenatal diagnosis and pregnancy-related genetic conditions.
Clinical specialty concerned with cancers occurring in children and young people.
Clinical specialty focused on the diagnosis and treatment of blood cancers.
Clinical specialty concerned with the diagnosis of disease through examination of cells, tissues and organs.
Clinical specialty concerned with genetic and congenital conditions affecting physical, cognitive or behavioural development.
